description:
This grid-based chart displays a 10x10 matrix of cells using a colour gradient to represent intensity values, ranging from deep blue (lowest intensity) through lighter blues and yellow to red (highest intensity). The central cell of the grid exhibits the highest intensity, indicated by its red colour. This central red cell is surrounded by four yellow cells in the cardinal directions (up, down, left, right). Further outwards, these yellow cells are flanked by light blue/cyan cells, which transition to lighter blue, and then to deep blue cells, forming a cross-like pattern of decreasing intensity radiating from the center.
The chart includes triangular markers on specific cells. One red triangle is positioned within the central red cell. Four orange triangles are located in the four yellow cells directly adjacent to the central red cell. An additional four orange triangles are situated in deep blue cells at the approximate mid-points of the grid's outermost extents: one at the top, one at the bottom, one on the left, and one on the right.
